title: Chitradurga Fort
tagline: Chitradurga Fort — heritage city of Karnataka

overview.short: Chitradurga Fort, or as the British called it Chitaldoorg, is a fortification that straddles several hills and a peak overlooking a flat valley in the Chitradurga District, Karnata
overview.description: Chitradurga Fort, or as the British called it Chitaldoorg, is a fortification that straddles several hills and a peak overlooking a flat valley in the Chitradurga District, Karnataka, India. The fort's name Chitrakaldurga, which means 'picturesque fort' in Kannada, is the namesake of the town Chitradurga and its administrative district. The fort was built in stages between the 11th and 13th centuries by the dynastic rulers of the region, including the Chalukyas and Hoysalas, later the Nayakas of Chitradurga of the Vijayanagar Empire. topPlaces.name: Jogimatti Wildlife Sanctuary

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chitradurga Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chitradurga Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chitradurga Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Bellary Airport (118 km) and the nearest railway station is Hubballi Junction (185 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chitradurga Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chitradurga Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chitradurga Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Chitradurga Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chitradurga Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bharamasagara, Turuvanur, Doddasiddavvanahalli, Dyamavvanahalli, Matadakurubarahatti.
